title: NIS2 Network Security Monitoring Coverage by Segment

compliance.assurance_rationale: Auto-migrated from cat-22 markdown on Phase 1.3 run. Requires SME review to confirm clause accuracy, mode (satisfies vs detects-violation-of), and assurance level (contributing/partial/full).

app: Splunk Enterprise Security (Splunkbase 263), Splunk Add-on for Stream (Splunkbase 1809)
spl:
| inputlookup nis2_segment_coverage_expectations.csv
| join segment_id [
    | tstats `summariesonly` count where index=netfw earliest=-24h by index
    | eval segment_id=case(
        match(index,"(?i)corp"),"CORP",
        match(index,"(?i)dmz"),"DMZ",
        1=1,"OTHER")
    | stats sum(count) as events by segment_id
]
| eval expected_eps=expected_events_per_day/86400*3600
| eval coverage_gap=if(events < expected_eps*0.5, 1, 0)
| where coverage_gap=1
| table segment_id, events, expected_eps, nis2_scope
value: Network security policies must be enforced uniformly. This use case measures log source coverage and IDS inspection rates per network segment defined in the security architecture — identifying blind spots where NIS2-scoped traffic is not visible to Splunk.
implementation: (1) Define minimum event rates per segment from baselines; (2) tag segments as in-scope for NIS2; (3) alert when collectors fail or indexes stop receiving; (4) integrate with ES data source monitoring; (5) quarterly architecture review of blind spots.
visualization: Table (segments with gaps), Single value (blind segments), Heatmap (EPS by hour), Map (site coverage).
cimSpl: | tstats summariesonly=t sum(All_Traffic.bytes_in) as agg_value from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic by All_Traffic.action, All_Traffic.src, All_Traffic.dest, All_Traffic.dest_port | sort - agg_value
knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
